channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: The Boring Stuff is Dangerous Now
item.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt acc...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
item.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Windows 'MiniPlasma' zero-day exploit gives SYSTEM access, PoC released
item.description: A cybersecurity researcher has released a proof-of-concept exploit for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day dubbed "MiniPlasma" that lets attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows s...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
item.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit now supports device-code phishing attacks and abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 Exploited in the Wild, Causing Worker Crashes and Possible RCE
item.description: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. The vulnerability, tracked...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
item.description:
Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase.
"Our investigation has deter...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft rejects critical Azure vulnerability report, no CVE issued
item.description: A security researcher claims Microsoft quietly fixed an Azure Backup for AKS vulnerability after rejecting his report, and without issuing a CVE. Microsoft disputes the claim, telling BleepingComputer...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
item.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Russian hackers turn Kazuar backdoor into modular P2P botnet
item.description: The Russian hacker group Secret Blizzard has developed its long-running Kazuar backdoor into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet designed for long-term persistence, stealth, and data collection. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Funnel Builder WordPress plugin bug exploited to steal credit cards
item.description: A critical vulnerability in the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress is being actively exploited to inject malicious JavaScript snippets into WooCommerce checkout pages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Exchange, Windows 11 hacked on second day of Pwn2Own
item.description: ​During the second day of Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, competitors collected $385,750 in cash awards after exploiting 15 unique zero-day vulnerabilities in multiple products, including Windows 11, Microsoft E...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Popular node-ipc npm package compromised to steal credentials
item.description: Hackers have injected credential-stealing malware into newly published versions of node-ipc, a popular inter-process communication package, in a new supply chain attack targeting npm. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Turla Turns Kazuar Backdoor Into Modular P2P Botnet for Persistent Access
item.description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacking group known as
Turla
has transformed its custom backdoor Kazuar into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet that's engineered for stealth and persistent ac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Avada Builder WordPress plugin flaws allow site credential theft
item.description: Two vulnerabilities in the Avada Builder plugin for WordPress, with an estimated one million active installations, allow hackers to read arbitrary files and extract sensitive information from the data...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft backpedals: Edge to stop loading passwords into memory
item.description: Microsoft is updating the Edge web browser to ensure it no longer loads saved passwords into process memory in clear text at startup after previously stating it was "by design." [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Inside the REMUS Infostealer: Session Theft, MaaS, and Rapid Evolution
item.description: Stolen browser sessions and authentication tokens are becoming more valuable than stolen passwords. Flare explains how the REMUS infostealer evolved around session theft and operational scalability. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Four OpenClaw Flaws Enable Data Theft, Privilege Escalation, and Persistence
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a set of four security flaws in OpenClaw that could be chained to achieve data theft, privilege escalation, and persistence.
The vulnerabilities, collectiv...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft to automatically roll back faulty Windows drivers
item.description: Microsoft is introducing a new capability that will allow it to remotely roll back problematic Windows drivers delivered through Windows Update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cyber Pioneers Ponder Past as Prologue
item.description: Robert "RSnake" Hansen, Katie Moussouris, Rich Mogull, Richard Stiennon, and Bruce Schneier reflect on how their favorite columns penned for Dark Reading over the past 20 years have stood the test of ...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware — It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks lik...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TanStack Supply Chain Attack Hits Two OpenAI Employee Devices, Forces macOS Updates
item.description: OpenAI has disclosed that two of its employee devices in its corporate environment were impacted via the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack on TanStack, but noted that no user data, production system...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft warns of Exchange zero-day flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: On Thursday, Microsoft shared mitigations for a high-severity Exchange Server vulnerability exploited in attacks that allow threat actors to execute arbitrary code via cross-site scripting (XSS) while...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: On-Prem Microsoft Exchange Server CVE-2026-42897 Exploited via Crafted Email
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed a new security vulnerability impacting on-premise versions of Exchange Server that it said has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-202...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
item.description: The U.S.Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly disclosed vulnerability impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
item.description: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: TeamPCP hackers advertise Mistral AI code repos for sale
item.description: The TeamPCP hacker group is threatening to leak source code from the Mistral AI project unless a buyer is found for the data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers exploit auth bypass flaw in Burst Statistics WordPress plugin
item.description: Hackers are leveraging a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Burst Statistics to obtain admin-level access to websites. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
item.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
item.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
item.description: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
